subject: UX & Research
title: Qualitative vs. Quantitative Balance
description: Learn Qualitative vs. Quantitative Balance in one minute.

hook.text: Numbers say what happened. Stories say why. You need both to make good product decisions.

buildup.text: Quantitative data scales but lacks context. Qualitative data explains but doesn't generalize.

discovery.text: Spool says: 'Watching one user struggle teaches more than a thousand data points about average session time.'

twist.text: Teams that only use data miss user emotion. Teams that only observe can't measure impact or scale insights.

climax.text: Best practice: quantitative data identifies problems, qualitative research explains them. Alternate between the two.

punchline.text: Data finds the problem. Research explains it. Alternate between both.

quiz.question: How did Jared Spool describe the power of small qualitative studies?
options: Watching one user struggle teaches more than a thousand data points about averages
Qualitative studies should never be used without quantitative validation
Five interviews replace all analytics data
